mnemonics: W.E.E.P. for E-Waste: Websites, E-books, E-courses, Publications
keyPoints: Utilize official government and UNEP publications for authoritative data.
Online courses and webinars from platforms like Coursera offer specialized knowledge.
Academic journals and e-books provide in-depth research and case studies on management strategies.
commonMistakes: Relying solely on generic web searches instead of curated academic or governmental resources.
Overlooking the practical guides and toolkits provided by organizations like the Basel Convention.
Confusing consumer electronics recycling information with large-scale industrial e-waste management policy.
